_comment: Baseline ns/op for each microbenchmark. CI runs the benchmarks and asserts
measured <= baseline * tolerance_multiplier so an order-of-magnitude regression
fails the build but day-to-day runner noise doesn't.
Numbers were captured on Apple M-series, release build, libc++.
Re-baseline by running benchmarks/run_baseline.py and committing the new file.
Tolerance is intentionally generous because CI runners (especially shared-tenant
GitHub Actions runners) are 2-4x slower and noisier than developer machines.
